title: Mutual Indemnification (Standard Market)
text: Each party (the 'Indemnifying Party') shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the other party and its officers, directors, employees, and agents (collectively, 'Indemnified Parties') from and against any third-party claims, actions, proceedings, and suits, and all related liabilities, damages, settlements, penalties, fines, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) the Indemnifying Party's gross negligence or willful misconduct; (b) the Indemnifying Party's material breach of this Agreement; or (c) the Indemnifying Party's infringement or misappropriation of any third-party intellectual property rights. Neither party's indemnification obligations shall exceed the greater of (i) the total fees paid or payable by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim or (ii) USD $100,000.

title: One-Sided Unlimited Indemnification (High Risk Pattern)
text: Customer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Vendor and its affiliates, officers, directors, employees, agents, and successors from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or related to: (a) Customer's use of the Services; (b) any breach by Customer of any representation, warranty, or obligation under this Agreement; (c) any third-party claims related to Customer Data; or (d) any violation of applicable law by Customer. This indemnification obligation shall be unlimited in amount and shall survive termination of this Agreement.

title: Mutual Limitation of Liability (Market Standard)
text: IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, PUNITIVE, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS, REVENUE, DATA, BUSINESS OPPORTUNITIES, OR GOODWILL) ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. EACH PARTY'S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY TO THE OTHER FOR ALL CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF: (A) THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CUSTOMER TO VENDOR IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTH PERIOD IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO LIABILITY; OR (B) USD $50,000. THE LIMITATIONS IN THIS SECTION SHALL NOT APPLY TO: (I) EITHER PARTY'S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS; (II) BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY; (III) DATA PROTECTION VIOLATIONS; (IV) GROSS NEGLIGENCE OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT; OR (V) EITHER PARTY'S INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS.

title: No Liability Cap (Critical Risk)
text: Neither party excludes or limits any liability that cannot be limited by law. All other liability is limited to direct losses only. There is no cap on the total aggregate liability of either party under this Agreement.

title: Mutual Termination with Cure Period (Market Standard)
text: Either party may terminate this Agreement (a) for convenience upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party; (b) immediately upon written notice if the other party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receiving written notice of such breach; (c) immediately if the other party becomes insolvent, makes a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, files or has filed against it a petition in bankruptcy, or has a receiver appointed for its business. Upon termination: (i) all licenses granted hereunder shall immediately terminate; (ii) each party shall promptly return or destroy the other party's Confidential Information; (iii) all fees accrued prior to termination shall remain due and payable.

title: At-Will Employment Termination
text: Employment with the Company is at-will, meaning that either the Employee or the Company may terminate the employment relationship at any time, for any reason or no reason, with or without cause, and with or without notice, subject to the terms of this Agreement. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to alter the at-will nature of employment or create any implied contract of employment for a definite period.

title: Balanced IP Assignment with Background IP Protection
text: Work Product. Contractor hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to all work product specifically created by Contractor for Client under this Agreement ('Work Product'), including all intellectual property rights therein. Background IP. Contractor retains all right, title, and interest in and to any pre-existing intellectual property, tools, methodologies, know-how, or technologies owned or developed by Contractor prior to or independent of this Agreement ('Background IP'). Contractor grants Client a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual license to use Background IP to the extent incorporated into or necessary to use the Work Product. California Labor Code Exception. In accordance with California Labor Code Section 2870, this Agreement does not apply to any invention that the Contractor developed entirely on their own time without using Company equipment, supplies, facilities, or trade secret information, and which does not relate to the Company's business or research and development.

title: Overbroad IP Assignment (High Risk for Contractor)
text: Employee/Contractor hereby irrevocably assigns to Company all inventions, discoveries, developments, improvements, trade secrets, and works of authorship conceived, made, developed, or reduced to practice by Employee/Contractor, whether alone or jointly with others, during the term of this Agreement, whether or not during working hours, whether or not related to Company's business, and whether or not using Company resources. This assignment includes all intellectual property rights worldwide and shall survive termination of this Agreement.

title: Non-Compete (VOID in California)
text: During the term of employment and for a period of two (2) years following termination of employment for any reason, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in, own, manage, operate, control, be employed by, provide services to, participate in, or be connected with any business or enterprise that competes with the Company's business within any geographic area where the Company conducts or has conducted business.
legal_note: This clause is VOID and UNENFORCEABLE in California under Business and Professions Code Section 16600. California employers cannot enforce non-compete agreements against employees.

title: Non-Solicitation (Potentially Enforceable in California)
text: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months following termination for any reason, Employee agrees not to solicit, recruit, or hire any employee of the Company who was employed by the Company during the last six months of Employee's employment. This restriction applies only to direct solicitation and does not prevent Employee from working at a company that independently recruits Company employees without Employee's involvement.

title: Mutual Confidentiality with Standard Exceptions
text: Each party ('Receiving Party') agrees to hold in strict confidence all Confidential Information of the other party ('Disclosing Party') and not to disclose or use any Confidential Information except as necessary to perform its obligations under this Agreement. 'Confidential Information' means any non-public information disclosed by one party to the other that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure. Exceptions. The obligations above do not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly known through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was rightfully known by the Receiving Party before disclosure; (c) is rightfully obtained from a third party without restriction; (d) is independently developed without using Confidential Information; or (e) is required to be disclosed by law or court order, provided the Receiving Party gives prompt notice to allow the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order. Term: Two (2) years from the date of disclosure, except for trade secrets which are protected in perpetuity.

title: California Governing Law (Standard)
text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. The parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in San Francisco County, California for any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Each party waives any objection to such jurisdiction or venue.

title: Mandatory Arbitration with Class Action Waiver
text: Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by JAMS pursuant to its Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures. The arbitration shall be conducted by a single arbitrator and shall take place in San Francisco, California. The arbitrator shall have authority to award any remedy available at law or in equity. EACH PARTY WAIVES ITS RIGHT TO A JURY TRIAL AND TO PARTICIPATE IN A CLASS ACTION. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may seek emergency injunctive relief in a court of competent jurisdiction to prevent irreparable harm pending arbitration.

title: Data Protection (GDPR/CCPA Compliant)
text: Data Processing. To the extent that either party processes Personal Data on behalf of the other party under this Agreement, such processing shall be subject to a separate Data Processing Agreement ('DPA') which is incorporated herein by reference. Security. Each party shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect Personal Data against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction, consistent with industry best practices and applicable law. Breach Notification. Each party shall notify the other party without undue delay, and in any event within seventy-two (72) hours, upon becoming aware of any Personal Data breach affecting the other party's Personal Data. Data Deletion. Upon termination of this Agreement, each party shall promptly delete or return all Personal Data belonging to the other party, unless retention is required by applicable law. CCPA Compliance. Vendor shall not sell, rent, or lease Customer Personal Data, and shall process such data only as a Service Provider under the California Consumer Privacy Act.

title: Force Majeure (Balanced, Standard)
text: Neither party shall be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement to the extent such delay or failure is caused by circumstances beyond its reasonable control, including without limitation: acts of God, natural disasters, pandemic or epidemic declared by a government authority, war, terrorism, civil unrest, governmental actions, power failures, or internet disruptions ('Force Majeure Event'). The affected party shall: (a) promptly notify the other party in writing of the Force Majeure Event; (b) use commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate the effects of the Force Majeure Event and resume performance; (c) provide regular updates on the status of the Force Majeure Event. If a Force Majeure Event continues for more than sixty (60) days, either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice without liability, except for payment of fees for services already performed.

title: Net 30 Payment Terms (Market Standard)
text: Customer shall pay all undisputed invoices within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Invoices shall be submitted monthly in arrears. Late payments shall accrue interest at the rate of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month (or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, whichever is less) from the due date until paid. If any invoice is disputed in good faith, Customer shall notify Vendor in writing within fifteen (15) days of receipt, specifying the basis for the dispute, and shall pay any undisputed amounts by the due date. The parties shall work together in good faith to resolve disputed amounts within thirty (30) days.

title: SaaS Service Warranty (Market Standard)
text: Vendor warrants that: (a) the Services will perform materially in accordance with the applicable Documentation; (b) Vendor will implement commercially reasonable security measures to protect Customer Data; (c) the Services will be provided in a professional manner consistent with industry standards; and (d) Vendor has the right and authority to enter into this Agreement and grant the licenses herein. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH HEREIN, THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED 'AS IS' AND VENDOR DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT. Warranty Remedy: If the Services fail to conform to the warranty above and Vendor fails to cure such failure within thirty (30) days of written notice, Customer's sole remedy shall be to terminate the applicable Order Form and receive a pro-rata refund of prepaid fees.

title: Mutual Representations and Warranties
text: Each party represents and warrants to the other party that: (a) it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of incorporation or formation; (b) it has the full right, power, and authority to enter into, execute, and perform its obligations under this Agreement; (c) the execution and performance of this Agreement does not violate any other agreement to which it is a party; (d) it will comply with all applicable laws and regulations in connection with its performance under this Agreement; and (e) it has obtained and will maintain all permits, licenses, and governmental approvals necessary for its performance under this Agreement.

title: NDA Purpose Limitation Clause
text: Each Receiving Party agrees to use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating and engaging in discussions concerning a potential business relationship between the parties (the 'Purpose') and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Receiving Party shall not reverse engineer, disassemble, or decompile any prototypes, software, or other tangible objects that embody the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall limit disclosure of Confidential Information to those of its employees and advisors who have a need to know for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations no less protective than those in this Agreement.

title: Employment Severance Provision
text: If the Company terminates Employee's employment without Cause (as defined below), and Employee executes a general release of claims in a form acceptable to the Company, Employee shall be entitled to: (a) continuation of Employee's base salary for a period of [X] months following the termination date; (b) continuation of Employee's health benefits for the same period; and (c) accelerated vesting of any unvested equity awards that would have vested in the [X] months following termination. 'Cause' means: (i) conviction of a felony; (ii) willful fraud or dishonesty that damages the Company; (iii) willful refusal to perform lawful duties after written notice; or (iv) material breach of any agreement with the Company.

title: Assignment (Consent Required)
text: Neither party may assign or transfer this Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may assign this Agreement without consent to: (a) an affiliate; or (b) a successor entity in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets, provided the assignee agrees in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Any purported assignment in violation of this Section shall be null and void.
